heading: Johnburgh vs Julanka Holdings — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Johnburgh, South Australia and Julanka Holdings, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Johnburgh has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Julanka Holdings (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Johnburgh or Julanka Holdings safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Johnburgh has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Julanka Holdings scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Johnburgh scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Julanka Holdings scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Johnburgh or Julanka Holdings?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Johnburgh has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
